title: Dell Scholars Program
body: Up to $20,000 for first-generation, low-income students. Includes laptop, resources, and a support community. One of the most comprehensive student awards.

title: Society of Women Engineers Scholarship
body: $1,000–$15,000 for women studying engineering or STEM. Multiple awards available. Membership in SWE adds networking value on top of the scholarship.

title: Google Generation Scholarship
body: For women and underrepresented groups in computer science. $10,000 award plus invitation to the Google Scholars Retreat.

title: Actuarial Foundation Scholarship
body: For students pursuing actuarial science, mathematics, or statistics with a finance or insurance career goal. Strong essay component.

title: NAACP HBCU Scholars Program
body: For Black students pursuing STEM, business, or policy. Includes mentorship, networking, and internship placement support alongside the scholarship.

title: Hispanic Scholarship Fund
body: For Hispanic and Latinx students pursuing any undergraduate major. Award is renewable and includes career development resources.

title: Jack Kent Cooke Foundation Scholarship
body: For high-achieving students with financial need. One of the largest private scholarships in the US, with extensive support services and community.

title: Women in Cybersecurity Scholarship
body: For women pursuing cybersecurity or information assurance degrees. WiCyS also offers a conference scholarship and mentorship program.
